proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, feature_051 is associated with shorter pfs_months: patients with feature_051=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with feature_051=0.
Most other binary features (e.g., feature_109, feature_039, feature_043) show only modest univariate associations with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test of pfs_months between feature_051=1 (n=27,481) vs feature_051=0 (n=22,519): mean difference = -0.521 months (3.50 vs 4.02). This is the strongest single binary signal in the cohort.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df[c]==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df[c]==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Across all 77 binary features, only feature_051 reached p<1e-100 univariately. feature_109 (effect +0.10, p=2e-3) and feature_039 (effect +0.094, p=3e-3) were the next strongest but with tiny univariate effects; most other binaries were null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (range 30-90, likely an age-like variable) is positively correlated with pfs_months: higher feature_078 predicts longer pfs_months.
feature_013 (range 0.12-3622, heavy right tail) is negatively correlated with pfs_months: higher feature_013 predicts shorter pfs_months (consistent with a tumor-burden / PSA-like marker).
feature_006 (range 0-24.6) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_009 (range 1.5-5.5) is positively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = +0.853, p < 1e-300. feature_078 is by far the most predictive single variable: mean pfs_months rises monotonically across age bins from 0.09 (30-40) to 7.20 (80-90).
Spearman rho = -0.223, p < 1e-300. Higher feature_013 robustly predicts shorter pfs_months.
Spearman rho = -0.134, p = 3.7e-200. Modest but highly significant negative correlation.
Spearman rho = +0.118, p = 1.4e-154. Higher feature_009 (1.5-5.5 range) modestly predicts longer pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3 levels: 0,1,2) is strongly associated with pfs_months in a graded fashion: each higher level corresponds to shorter pfs_months, consistent with a stage / risk-group variable.
Race (feature_018: white/black/hispanic/asian/other) has a univariate effect on pfs_months.
Insurance (feature_045: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured) has a univariate effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA F=4445.9, p<<1e-300. Mean pfs_months: level 0=4.68, level 1=3.49, level 2=2.38 — large monotonic decrease.
ANOVA F=0.56, p=0.69. Mean pfs_months by race ranges only 3.74-3.80; no univariate race effect.
ANOVA F=0.34, p=0.79. Mean pfs_months ranges only 3.73-3.75; no univariate insurance effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS regression with feature_078, feature_051, feature_057 (as 2 dummies), feature_013, feature_006, and feature_009 will jointly explain a very large share of variance in pfs_months and each predictor will retain an independent signed effect consistent with its univariate direction.

analyses.result_summary: OLS R^2 = 0.946. Adjusted coefficients: feature_078 = +0.174 mo/yr (t=822); feature_051 = -0.376 mo (t=-85); feature_057 level 1 = -1.18 mo, level 2 = -2.29 mo; feature_013 = -0.0027 per unit (t=-114); feature_006 = -0.076 per unit (t=-140); feature_009 = +0.479 per unit (t=114). All p<<1e-50 and all sign-consistent with univariate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the seven main covariates from iteration 4, feature_109 retains a positive independent effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment, feature_039 retains a positive independent effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment, feature_027 has a small positive independent effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment, feature_043 has a small negative independent effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coef for feature_109 = +0.156 months, p = 7.1e-110. Robust positive independent effect not visible univariately.
Adjusted coef for feature_039 = +0.147 months, p = 2.5e-99. Mirror image of feature_109 in magnitude and direction.
Adjusted coef = +0.022 months, p = 4.0e-7. Small but precisely-estimated positive adjusted effect.
Adjusted coef = -0.022 months, p = 4.0e-5. Small adjusted negative effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the base seven covariates, feature_092 (continuous, range 48-830) has a small independent negative association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coef for feature_092 = -0.000496 per unit, p = 2.8e-81. Highly significant although the per-unit slope is small. Across the empirical range it implies ~0.4-month variation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (5 levels 6-10, plausibly a Gleason-grade variable) retains a small adjusted effect on pfs_months even after the base 7 covariates.
Race (feature_018) has no independent effect on pfs_months after adjustment for clinical covariates.
Insurance (feature_045) has no independent effect on pfs_months after adjustment for clinical covariates.

analyses.code: OLS adjusting for base 7; joint F-test on feature_067 dummies
analyses.result_summary: Joint F on feature_067 dummies = 11.31, p = 3.5e-9. Coefficients (vs level 6 reference): level 7 +0.008 (ns), 8 -0.020 (p=0.005), 9 -0.022 (p=0.010), 10 -0.023 (p=0.044). Modest graded negative effect at the high-grade end.

analyses.code: OLS adjusting for base 7 + race dummies + insurance dummies
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients (reference white): black -0.001 (p=0.87), hispanic +0.0005 (p=0.94), asian +0.005 (p=0.56), other -0.004 (p=0.81). All effectively zero — no race disparity in pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: OLS adjusting for base 7 + insurance dummies
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted insurance coefficients (reference private): medicaid -0.008 (p=0.22), medicare +0.001 (p=0.82), uninsured -0.0002 (p=0.99). No insurance disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 and feature_039 interact synergistically: patients with both feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1 have substantially longer pfs_months than additive single-positive effects would predict, consistent with a paired predictive-biomarker pattern.

analyses.code: OLS with base 7 + feature_109 + feature_039 + (feature_109 * feature_039)
analyses.result_summary: Cell means (raw pfs_months): 109=0,039=0 -> 3.74 (n=40,435); 109=0,039=1 -> 3.67 (n=4,569); 109=1,039=0 -> 3.67 (n=4,467); 109=1,039=1 -> 5.22 (n=529). In the adjusted model the main effects of feature_109 and feature_039 collapse to ~0; the multiplicative interaction term carries +1.44 months (t=66.6, p<<1e-300). Pure synergy: neither alone helps, but both together produce ~1.5 months of additional PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_109 x feature_039 synergy is itself amplified by feature_027: patients with all three positive (109=1, 039=1, 027=1) experience an even larger PFS benefit, indicating a three-way interaction.

analyses.code: OLS with base 7 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_027 + all 2-way interactions + triple interaction
analyses.result_summary: Cell means (raw pfs_months) for (109, 039, 027): (1,1,0) -> 3.83 (n=199); (1,1,1) -> 6.05 (n=330). With all main effects and 2-way interactions in the model, the triple-interaction coefficient is +2.26 months (t=52.5, p<<1e-300). Adding it lifts model R^2 from 0.952 to 0.955. The 2-way 109x039, 109x027, 039x027 terms become non-significant once the triple is included — the entire synergy lives in the triple-positive subgroup (~0.7% of the cohort).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is modified by feature_057 (stage): the absolute treatment-related decrement is smaller (less negative) at higher feature_057 levels.

analyses.result_summary: feature_051 main effect at feature_057=0: -0.397 months. Interaction coefs: feature_051*feature_057_1 = +0.026 (p=0.006); feature_051*feature_057_2 = +0.056 (p=2e-5). The decrement attenuates from ~-0.40 (level 0) to ~-0.34 (level 2). Statistically robust but small relative to the main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is modified by feature_013 (high tumor-burden marker): the negative effect of feature_051 is smaller in patients with higher feature_013.
The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is amplified in older patients (higher feature_078).
The effect of feature_051 is modified by feature_009: the negative feature_051 effect is slightly larger in patients with higher feature_009.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.0153 per unit feature_013 (t large, p<<1e-300). High-feature_013 patients see less negative impact from feature_051. The sign indicates feature_013 attenuates the deleterious effect.
Interaction coef = -0.00426 per year (p=2.7e-24). The negative feature_051 effect grows by ~0.04 mo for every 10-year increase in feature_078 — small but highly significant.
Interaction coef = -0.0170 per unit feature_009 (p=0.041). Marginally significant; suggests slightly larger feature_051 decrement in patients with higher feature_009.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The probability of being assigned feature_051=1 differs by race (feature_018), suggesting differential treatment allocation.
The probability of being assigned feature_051=1 differs by insurance category (feature_045).

analyses.result_summary: feature_051 prevalence by race: asian 0.549, black 0.554, hispanic 0.544, other 0.544, white 0.550. Chi-square p ~ 0.86 (range 1 percentage point). No allocation disparity by race.
feature_051 prevalence by insurance: medicaid 0.555, medicare 0.546, private 0.550, uninsured 0.562. Range 1.6 pp. Chi-square not significant. No allocation disparity by insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_051=1 patients, race independently predicts pfs_months after full clinical adjustment.
Within feature_051=0 patients, race independently predicts pfs_months after full clinical adjustment.

analyses.code: OLS within feature_051=1 subgroup including race + insurance dummies + all main covariates and feature_109/039/027
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients within feature_051=1 (reference asian): black +0.007 (p=0.62), hispanic -0.009 (p=0.53), other -0.008 (p=0.74), white -0.002 (p=0.89). Insurance also null. No within-treated disparity.

analyses.code: OLS within feature_051=0 subgroup with the same predictors
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients within feature_051=0 (reference asian): black -0.015 (p=0.22), hispanic -0.002 (p=0.86), other -0.016 (p=0.44), white -0.012 (p=0.25). All small and non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across racial groups (race x feature_051 interaction).

analyses.code: OLS with base predictors + race main effects + race*feature_051 interaction terms
analyses.result_summary: Stratified per-race feature_051 coefficients (adjusted): asian -0.36, black -0.36, white -0.37, hispanic -0.43, other -0.37. Formal interaction tests: race_black*feature_051 p=0.08; race_hispanic*feature_051 p=0.10; others NS. Hispanic patients show a hint of larger negative feature_051 effect, but no individual interaction reaches conventional significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (treatment-like) attenuates the feature_109 x feature_039 synergy: the synergistic PFS gain in double-positive patients is smaller among feature_051=1 patients than among feature_051=0 patients.

analyses.code: OLS with base 7 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_109*feature_039 + feature_051*(feature_109*feature_039)
analyses.result_summary: Raw cell means: among feature_051=0, double-positive (109=1,039=1) -> 5.56 vs 4.02 baseline (~1.6 mo gain); among feature_051=1, double-positive -> 4.95 vs 3.51 (~1.5 mo gain). The triple interaction coef is -0.112 months (p=0.004). Statistically reliable but modest attenuation: the synergy persists in both feature_051 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A final consolidated multivariable model that includes the seven base covariates, feature_109, feature_039, feature_027, feature_092, feature_043, the feature_067 (Gleason-like) grades, and the feature_109*feature_039*feature_027 triple interaction will explain ~95% or more of the variance in pfs_months without needing further individual feature additions.

analyses.code: OLS with all consolidated predictors + triple interaction; report adjusted R^2 and key coefficients
analyses.result_summary: Consolidated OLS: R^2 = 0.955. Key signed effects: feature_078 +0.174 mo/yr; feature_051 -0.376 mo; feature_057 levels -1.18 / -2.29 mo; feature_013 -0.0027/unit; feature_006 -0.076/unit; feature_009 +0.479/unit; feature_092 -0.0005/unit; feature_109 (alone) ~0; feature_039 (alone) ~0; feature_027 ~0; triple_109_039_027 +2.26 mo; feature_067 grade-10 vs grade-6 -0.023 mo; feature_043 -0.017 mo. Race and insurance contribute essentially nothing.
